steps.title: Find the shown leg
steps.content:
From the graph, the shown segment has horizontal change $6$ and vertical change $9$. Its length is
$$\sqrt{6^2+9^2}=\sqrt{117}=3\sqrt{13}$$

steps.title: Use the area formula
steps.content:
Let $L$ be the other leg. A right triangle's area is half the product of its legs.
$$\frac{1}{2}(3\sqrt{13})(L)=48\sqrt{13}$$

steps.title: Solve for the other leg
steps.content: Multiply by $2$ and divide by $$3\sqrt{13}$$ $$L=\frac{96\sqrt{13}}{3\sqrt{13}}=32$$ The answer is B.
